pages.full_text:
On to __San Feliciano__ on __Lake Trasimeno__. Lovely sunshine, but wind too cold to sit outside.
Another stunning view. We are parked next to Belgians who have been coming to this lake for 40 years.
__Friday__ sunshine again. We watched a plane picking up water + dumping it. We thought it was fire extinguishing practice, but the Belgian says it's water pollution testing for Univ. __Perugia__/[?Perugua]. An odd bird here - horizontal black + white stripes + a red hat [sketch of bird]

We had a lazy morning 'reading by the lake. Simon cycled up the road to buy bread. At 3.30. we left for [?__Castelggioni del Mare__] [?Castiglione del Lago]. The drive there was beautiful - fertile fields. Grain + olives. Several abandoned houses. We reached the town + walked along the mainstreet - very pretty, full of tourist shops. The castle was interesting - frescoes + a Picasso exhibition. We walked around the
pages.notes: Text starts mid-sentence, continuing from previous page. The town name is difficult to read — the handwriting appears to show 'Castelggioni del Mare' or similar, but this is almost certainly Castiglione del Lago, the well-known town on Lake Trasimeno with a famous castle. The word after 'del' could be 'Lago' written quickly. The mention of Univ. Perugia is partially obscured. A small sketch of a bird (horizontal black & white stripes, red hat) appears in the text. Vertical marginalia on right edge reads 'Tramonto sull'Isola Polvese - Lake Trasimeno' — likely a postcard caption. No year visible on this page; volume B covers 2003–2019.

pages.notes: Page is mostly occupied by a large glued-in postcard photograph of Lago Trasimeno (sunset/dusk scene over water with reeds). Printed text on postcard reads 'LAGO TRASIMENO'. Vertical handwritten caption in left margin reads 'Tramonto sull'Isola Polvese - Lake Trasimeno'. The written text at the top continues from the left page and ends mid-sentence ('We picked up'), continuing onto the next spread. The Italian phrase 'non sporgersi' means 'do not lean out'.
